justification: All pass criteria met: 7+ failure modes identified (cascading failure, double charging, silent notification loss, inconsistent state, retry storm, partial failure visibility, zombie orders). Phantom charge risk explicitly identified and addressed with idempotency keys and reconciliation. Fire-and-forget notification weakness thoroughly addressed with transactional outbox pattern. Circuit breaker and timeout patterns recommended with specific parameters. Idempotency keys for payment retries specified with deterministic derivation from orderId. Trade-offs for added complexity acknowledged across three detailed decision analyses.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough failure mode enumeration — 7 distinct risks identified with severity ratings, well beyond the 5 minimum
Phantom charge / double-charging risk addressed with both idempotency keys and a reconciliation job as a safety net
Fire-and-forget replacement uses transactional outbox pattern, correctly identifying the dual-write problem with naive message broker solutions
Implementation guidance is phased by priority with specific file paths and concrete pitfalls to avoid
Distinguishes between transient failures (retry with backoff) and permanent failures (don't retry 4xx) — avoids the rubric failure mode of recommending retries for non-idempotent operations
Zombie orders scenario (deadline propagation) goes beyond standard failure modes — shows deep distributed systems understanding
Trade-offs section weighs synchronous vs async, outbox vs direct publish, and orchestration vs choreography with alternatives considered

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 8 failure modes identified (well above 5 minimum); phantom charge risk explicitly identified as FAILURE MODE 2 (double charging) and FAILURE MODE 3 (payment succeeds but order crashes); fire-and-forget notification weakness thoroughly addressed with transactional outbox pattern; circuit breaker and timeout patterns recommended with specific parameters (5s, 10s, 1s connect); idempotency keys specified at every boundary; trade-offs for added complexity acknowledged across three detailed trade-off entries.

observations.text: Exceptional failure mode coverage — 8 distinct modes including the subtle Stripe 429 vs 5xx discrimination that most analyses miss
Phantom charge addressed from two angles: retry-induced double charge (FM2) and crash-induced inconsistent state (FM3), with distinct mitigations for each
Fire-and-forget replacement is thorough — transactional outbox with same-transaction guarantee, polling publisher, and DLQ for Notification Service internal failures
Implementation guidance is phased and actionable with specific file paths, library suggestions, and a clear pitfalls-to-avoid list
Correctly warns against retrying without idempotency keys — directly avoids a key failure mode from the rubric
Bulkhead pattern recommended to prevent payment failures from starving order reads — goes beyond basic circuit breaker advice
Implementation guidance mentions patterns to follow but doesn't specify concrete retry parameters (e.g., 3 retries at 100ms/500ms/1000ms) — the rubric expected specific backoff values

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 9 failure modes identified (well above 5 minimum). Phantom charge risk explicitly covered in FM2 (timeout double-charge) and FM3 (lost response, charged but no order) and FM5 (crash after payment). Fire-and-forget notification weakness addressed with transactional outbox pattern. Circuit breaker and timeout patterns recommended with specific budget guidance. Idempotency keys for payment retries specified with Stripe forwarding. Trade-offs for added complexity acknowledged across 4 detailed trade-off entries.

observations.text: 9 distinct failure modes identified with specific mitigations for each — exceeds the 5 minimum significantly
Phantom charge risk covered from multiple angles: timeout retry (FM2), lost response (FM3), and crash after payment (FM5)
Fire-and-forget explicitly replaced with transactional outbox pattern, including implementation details (outbox table, poller/CDC, same-transaction write)
Idempotency keys specified with practical detail — including Stripe's 24-hour window limitation
Circuit breaker pattern includes half-open state and thundering herd mitigation (FM8) — goes beyond basic recommendation
Distinguishes retryable (network timeout) from non-retryable (card declined) failures in pitfalls
Phased implementation guidance with clear prioritization — idempotency keys first as highest ROI
Timeout budget math correctly noted: upstream timeout must exceed downstream timeout
Reconciliation job for orphaned payments addresses the gap between eventual consistency and correctness
No specific retry parameters (e.g., 3 retries, 100ms/500ms/1000ms backoff) as expected by the rubric — mentions exponential backoff with jitter but no concrete values
